sources.notes: Grid completion problem: turn white cells black so all black cells are connected and no horizontal/vertical run exceeds length 3.
Luogu solution classifies all possible connected shapes under the run-length constraint as a 2x2 square or two adjacent diagonals, then brute-forces containment.

annotation.summary: Decide whether the existing black cells can be extended to one connected black component with no 4 consecutive black cells horizontally or vertically.
annotation.constraints: n is only 100, so enumerating O(n) diagonal bands and O(n^2) squares is safe.
annotation.core_idea: The local run-length restriction is so strong that any connected completed shape must be either a 2x2 square or two adjacent diagonals of slope +1 or -1. Therefore the original black cells are feasible iff they are all contained in one of those candidate shapes; empty input and n=1 are special easy YES cases.
tricks: cover all existing black cells by a candidate shape

solution_variants.name: shape-classification-bruteforce
solution_variants.summary: Enumerate every 2x2 square and every pair of adjacent diagonals in both slopes, checking whether all existing black cells lie inside.

tags.evidence: After the shape classification, all possible candidate shapes are enumerated directly.
The solution is mostly careful enumeration of diagonal/square cases and edge cases.
The feasibility condition is about one 4-neighbour connected component, although no graph traversal is needed after classification.
